source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Consider a binary digital communication system which uses pulses \( g(t) \) and \( -g(t) \) for transmitting bits over an AWGN channel. If the receiver uses a matched filter, which one of the following pulses will give the minimum probability of bit error?
extraction_notes: image field is a PIL.Image object placeholder, not a path or URL
raw_answer_text mapped from solution letter A to option text

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the core information is visual

question asks which pulse gives minimum bit error, implying comparison among visual pulse candidates rather than text alone
asset registry shows an image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving requires applying matched-filter/AWGN theory to determine the pulse property linked to minimum bit error
then the solver must compare multiple candidate graphs to identify which visual pulse best matches that criterion
there are 4 choices, so this is not a direct lookup and involves theory-plus-image selection
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a discrete multiple-choice style answer ('graph A') is easy to compare against expected output
the question has a finite option set (A/B/C/D), which supports rule-based checking
registry_passed=true, answer_present=true, and the image asset exists, so a downstream verifier can inspect the same visual evidence
